runIntent: Fresh 20-prompt audit of the reverted codebase used to disambiguate whether the post-clarification-fix contextBudget regression (-0.35 reviewer / -0.55 analyzer) was fix-attributable or sample variance.

controlAttributionOutcome.verdictReason: Under both analyzer and reviewer scoring, the control on the same reverted code lands within 0.10 of the post-fix mean and is 0.45 below the pre-experiment mean. The pre-experiment run was the outlier (only 1 truncation event, q21 at 142KB); the post-fix and control runs each have 4-5 truncation events that differ only in which specific traces trip the truncation limit. q21 itself truncates at a different size in each run (pre=142KB, post=36KB, control=190KB), confirming run-to-run payload-size variance is the dominant driver. None of the truncations in the post-fix run were caused by the fix's decisionSignal (searchFocused_gate_overridden_by_dominant_fit_score); the control replicates the truncation pattern without the fix. The -0.35 reviewer delta from pre to post is therefore sample variance, not a fix-attributable regression.
controlAttributionOutcome.disambiguatedRejectionRecommendation: re-run decision with control as the new pre baseline and ship the fix

calibrationContext.note: Control finalScoreMean (4.62) is the highest of the three runs, above the 6-prompt regression baseline (4.5). The elevation vs pre is driven almost entirely by three traces (q5, q9, q20) that routed correctly in the control but not in the pre-experiment run, even on the same reverted code. Those three traces do NOT exhibit the viable_pool_excludes_top_fit_candidate pattern in the control, so the 'pre-experiment 5-trace cluster' was partly amplified by one-off nondeterministic routing, not a stable 5-trace structural cluster.

controlVsPostFixDeltas.note: Read this block as 'control (proposed new pre-baseline) vs post-fix'. Clarification +0.15 is the target-dimension lift from the fix when measured against the proper baseline. No hard-regression threshold is tripped. The -0.25 finalScore delta is attributable almost entirely to q16's post-fix timeout (single flaky trace that succeeded in both pre and control); excluding q16 from both runs leaves finalScore delta approximately -0.08.

weakestDimension.name: contextBudget
weakestDimension.confidenceReason: contextBudget is the lowest-mean dimension but its low score is dominated by broad-search / multi-step truncation on q16, q21, q23, q24, plus a modest-truncation q8 case. The cluster is stable across runs (5 traces in control, 4 in post, 1 in pre-experiment's unusually clean sample) but the per-trace membership varies with Kalshi payload size. Treat as a structural dimension with high per-run sampling noise.

rootCauseDimension.name: clarification_pool_construction_mixed_margin
rootCauseDimension.confidenceReason: Two cluster members (q1 margin 20.62, q21 margin 5.17) reproduce under the reverted code. q1 is the canonical dominant-fitScore override case (the lever the rolled-back fix targets); q21 is the small-margin residual subclass. q9 technical-only exclusion is semantically correct routing. Cluster size is stable at 2-3 traces/20 in this regime; the pre-experiment's 5-trace cluster was inflated by q5/q9/q20 nondeterministic mis-routing that does not reproduce in the control on the same code.

alternativeRootCauses.name: broad_search_payload_truncation
alternativeRootCauses.note: Five traces (q8, q16, q21, q23, q24) show canonicalBytes >= 24KB with truncated=true. Pattern is stable across runs but per-trace membership varies. Downstream of (a) Kalshi payload-size variance and (b) iterative-execution multi-step scans. Addressable at the canonical-dataset slicing layer rather than the clarification layer. Not a consequence of the reverted fix.

alternativeRootCauses.name: iterative_loop_budget_overrun
alternativeRootCauses.note: Four traces (q8, q16, q21, q24) completed 12 iterations against a stepBudget of 10. The overrun is compensatory (bad routing or broad-scan prompts) rather than planner-level. Downstream of other dimensions; not a direct lever.

analyzerDisagreements.reason: Analyzer's determineBottleneckStage heuristic (executionDurationMs/totalDurationMs >= 0.4) scored execution=2 on 20/20 traces because iterative-execution sums executionDurationMs across loops while totalDurationMs is wall clock; ratios are mathematically >100% on many traces (q8=586%, q9=288%, q12=457%, q16=528%, q21=648%, q24=435%). Raw trace evidence shows retryCount=0, selfHealCount=0 on all 20 traces; failureCount>0 only on q12 (1) and q24 (1). Reviewer rescored from raw failure/retry/loop-budget counts: score=4 when completedStepCount > stepBudget (compensatory overrun), else score=5. Gives execution cluster q8=4, q16=4, q21=4, q24=4, everything else 5.

Analyzer scored the two triggered clarifications (q1, q21) at 3 and everything else at 5. Reviewer lowered q1 to 2 (top-fit discover_trending_markets at fitScore 43.96 excluded from a pool of [analyze_market_liquidity 23.34, search_markets, get_market]; 20.62-point margin — wide-margin pool-construction failure, matches the canonical viable_pool_excludes_top_fit_candidate definition). Reviewer kept q21 at 3 (analyzer-aligned; same 5.17-point smaller-margin pattern the post-fix reviewer used). Lowered q9 from 5 to 4 because the pool technically excludes top-fit discover_trending_markets (22.18) in favor of search_markets (#3, 20.07), but the exclusion is semantically correct for a price-range-filter query. Net +0.40 vs pre reviewer (4.30) because q5, q9, q20 do not exhibit the cluster pattern in the control even though they did in the pre-experiment run.

Reviewer adjusted q8 (24KB, truncated, answer intact, finalScore 4.83), q23 (86KB, truncated, actionability down to 3 but answer complete, finalScore 4.5), and q24 (47KB, truncated on not-found fallback, answer correct, finalScore 4.83) from analyzer 2 to 3 — same rubric the post-fix reviewer used on q10/q23/q24 (truncation occurred but the final answer was not blocked). Kept q16 at 2 (631KB, significantly degraded answer explicitly flagged as partial) and q21 at 2 (190KB, 12 compensatory loops). Net reviewer mean 4.40 vs analyzer 4.25.

Overrode analyzer 3 on q12 (toolCallCount=8, no transport errors) and q20, q24 (call-count proxy fires on healthy iterative traces) — aligned with the post-fix reviewer's same correction. Kept q21 at 3 (loop amplification, 12 completed loops vs 10 budget, 182s wall clock — same pre-experiment methodology for q21). Kept q16 at 4 (24 tool calls, 203s wall clock; no transport failures but high call volume).

Analyzer scored synthesis=4 on q5, q10, q12, q19 (4 traces) and synthesis=5 elsewhere. Reviewer additionally lowered q16 (partial budget-exhausted answer, flagged incompleteness in response text), q21 (partial answer via 12 compensatory loops, actionability=3), and q23 (partial broad-scan, actionability=3) from 5 to 4. Kept everything else as analyzer. Total 7 sub-5 synthesis traces, 13 at 5.

toolSelection.reason: All 20 scored 5. Single-contributor eval does not exercise cross-contributor selection load.

scoutDepth.reason: probeConfidence 0.9894 on all 20 traces. No degraded scouting.

clarification.reason: Two reproducible cluster members (q1 wide margin, q21 small margin) replicate across both pre and control on the reverted code. q9 technical-only behavior reproduces across post and control. Cluster is stable; per-run membership of q5, q9, q20 varies with nondeterministic routing.

planning.reason: First-pass planning scored 5 everywhere. Iterative-loop budget overruns (q8, q16, q21, q24 all 12>10) are attributed to execution compensatory behavior rather than planning in current scoring.

execution.reason: Analyzer heuristic is the same broken latency-ratio metric flagged in pre and post reviews. Reviewer rescored from raw counters. Low confidence on absolute number; high confidence execution is NOT the weakest lever because retry/failure counts are near zero.

synthesis.reason: Mix of genuine terseness (q19) and downstream truncation-linked partial answers (q16, q21, q23). Underlying pattern stable; absolute mean drifts with which traces truncate in a given run.

contextBudget.reason: Cluster pattern is stable (broad-search / multi-step truncation) but per-trace membership varies with Kalshi market payload size. Control has 5 truncation events vs pre's 1 and post's 4 — same underlying system, different sampling on the same prompt set. Dimension needs n>=40 or multi-run averaging to score reliably.

reasoningDepth.reason: Mechanical reasoning adequate across all 20 traces; no low scores.

mcpTransport.reason: Analyzer call-count / duration proxy fires on healthy iterative-execution traces. Reviewer overrides same as prior reviews.

codeSafety.reason: validationApplied=false on all 20 traces (iterative mode). Not exercised.

synthesisBudget.reason: Five sub-5 traces (q8, q16, q21, q23, q24) — same set as contextBudget truncation cluster. Downstream dimension.

toolSelection.reason: Calibration-stable at 5.0. Not a useful direct lever on single-contributor eval.

clarification.reason: Control reproduces the two-member semantic cluster (q1 wide margin, q21 small margin) on the reverted code. q1 is the canonical target the rolled-back dominant-fitScore override was designed to handle. The reverted code's 4.70 clarification mean is the correct baseline for measuring the fix's lift; post-fix improves this to 4.85 (+0.15). Recommend re-running the decision script with control-as-pre and shipping the fix.

planning.reason: First-pass planning score ceiling at 5. Iterative-loop budget overruns are compensatory, not planning defects.

execution.reason: Analyzer heuristic remains broken. Reviewer scoring is robust but do not target execution as a primary lever until the determineBottleneckStage heuristic is clamped to [0,1] or replaced with a max-stage-elapsed metric.

synthesis.reason: Mean drifts with per-run truncation cluster membership. Structural terseness (q19) is a single-trace residual below cluster-significance. Not a direct lever this iteration.

contextBudget.reason: Stable structural cluster (broad-search/multi-step truncation) but per-trace membership has high run-to-run variance. Optimization target would be the canonical-dataset slicing layer rather than routing. Needs multi-run measurement (n>=40 or 3-run median) before committing. NOT fix-attributable from the post-fix run — control confirms noise.

reasoningDepth.reason: Mechanical reasoning is sufficient. No optimization needed.

mcpTransport.reason: Call-count/duration proxy is structurally noisy on iterative execution. Analyzer-side separation of tool_call_count from transport_health is the prerequisite for making this a useful dimension.

codeSafety.reason: Not exercised in current iterative mode.

synthesisBudget.reason: Downstream of contextBudget truncation cluster. Will move with the truncation story, not as a direct lever.

comparisonToPre.note: Three of the pre-experiment cluster members (q5, q9, q20) do NOT exhibit the cluster pattern in the control on the same reverted code. q5 and q20 have the top-fit candidate IN comparedOptionIds and SELECTED; q9 has a technical-only exclusion (semantically correct routing). This demonstrates that the pre-experiment 5-trace cluster size was partly amplified by nondeterministic routing on those three traces, not a stable 5-trace structural cluster. The true baseline cluster size on this prompt set is 2-3 traces per 20-prompt run.

comparisonToPostFix.note: Control to post-fix cluster shrinkage is 1 trace (50%). Absolute shrinkage does not meet the 2-trace minimum but percent shrinkage (50%) meets the 20% threshold. The fix's ceiling on this prompt set is exactly 1 cluster exit per run (q1); q21 is the known small-margin residual below the override threshold.

q9.semanticEval: top-fit was wrong for price-range-filter query; search_markets is correct. Count as technical-only, not semantic.

viable_pool_excludes_top_fit_candidate.whyItMatters: Two structurally reproducible pool-construction failure cases on the reverted code: (1) q1 wide-margin case (exactly what the rolled-back dominant-fitScore override was designed to fix); (2) q21 small-margin residual (below the override threshold by design). The structural cluster is 2 traces per run, NOT 5. Any decision script comparing post-fix cluster shrinkage should use this control as its baseline, not the pre-experiment run.

comparisonToPre.note: Pre had an unusually clean truncation sample (1 event). Control and post-fix have 5 and 4 respectively. Cluster IS structural; per-trace membership varies with Kalshi market payload size.

comparisonToPostFix.note: 3 of 5 traces overlap. Non-overlapping traces are driven by which broad-search / multi-step payloads happen to cross the truncation threshold in a given run. Not fix-attributable. Decisive evidence that the post-fix contextBudget regression is run-to-run variance.

q21PayloadSizeAcrossRuns.note: Same query, same code paths, 5.2x payload-size range across runs. Direct evidence of Kalshi market payload variance driving the contextBudget signal.

broad_search_payload_truncation.attributionEvidence: None of the control's 5 truncation traces were produced by the rolled-back fix (fix is reverted). The same pattern appears with 4 traces in post-fix (where fix was active but those 4 traces did not fire the override signal) and with 1 trace in pre (where fix was reverted, same as control). Control and post-fix truncation counts match far more closely than pre and post-fix counts do.
broad_search_payload_truncation.whyItMatters: Structural cluster addressable at the canonical-dataset slicing layer (page-size caps, per-step trimming) rather than routing. High run-to-run variance makes n=20 unreliable for this dimension. Use n>=40 or 3-run median.

iterative_loop_budget_overrun.description: completedStepCount > stepBudget on 4 traces (all 12 completed vs 10 budget). Compensatory execution driven by broad-scan prompts or smaller-margin residual routing. Downstream of the broad_search_payload_truncation cluster.

comparisonToPostFix.note: q16 differs: control completed 12 loops; post-fix timed out before any tool call. Same underlying watchdog/adaptation gap.

iterative_loop_budget_overrun.whyItMatters: Structural but downstream of routing. Addressable via planner/execution-loop paired fix, not a direct lever.

synthesis_terseness.note: Same carry-over pattern as pre and post. 175-char response with correct aggregates but no per-event breakdown. Below cluster-significance threshold.

calibrationNotes: Analyzer determineBottleneckStage heuristic (executionDurationMs/totalDurationMs >= 0.4) is unchanged from pre and post. On the control it scored execution=2 on 20/20 traces because iterative-execution sums executionDurationMs across loops. Strongly recommend clamping executionDurationShare to [0,1] before thresholding or switching to a max-stage-elapsed metric. The dimension is unusable in its current form.
Analyzer mcpTransport continues to fire call-count / duration proxy false positives on healthy iterative traces (q12, q20, q24 on control). Same calibration note as prior reviews.

Key calibration finding from this control: contextBudget truncation cluster membership has ~25-50% run-to-run drift on this prompt set due to Kalshi market payload-size variance. At n=20, the dimension mean can swing by 0.45 without any code change. Multi-run median or n>=40 is required for trustworthy contextBudget deltas.

Second calibration finding: viable_pool_excludes_top_fit_candidate cluster membership on 'mid-range' queries (q5, q9, q20) is also run-to-run variable due to nondeterministic routing. q5, q9, q20 can land on right or wrong tools without any code change, producing +/- 3 traces of cluster-size variance. The STABLE cluster members are q1 (wide margin) and q21 (small margin). Future cluster-shrinkage decisions should be weighted on the structural members (q1 + q21) and discount the variable-membership members.

Third calibration finding: q16 is a flaky broad-discovery trace. It completed in both pre (loop-repeat, finalScore 4.0) and control (12-loop completion, finalScore 4.5) but timed out in post-fix (finalScore 1.0) on the same prompt set, with no obvious causal link to the clarification fix. Recommend treating q16 as a flaky-exclusion candidate for noise-sensitive dimensions or measuring multi-run.
qualitativeSummary: The control audit, run on the same reverted code and same prompt set as the pre-experiment baseline, establishes that the post-clarification-fix's disputed contextBudget regression is sample variance, not fix-attributable. Reviewer-scored contextBudget for control (4.40) lands 0.10 below post-fix (4.50) and 0.45 below pre (4.85); the control has MORE truncation events (5) than post-fix (4) and far more than pre (1), and the fix is reverted in the control, so the truncation pattern cannot be caused by the fix. The root cause is Kalshi market payload-size variance, directly evidenced by q21's canonicalBytes ranging 36KB to 190KB across the three runs on the same query text. The same control run also reveals that the pre-experiment's 'viable_pool_excludes_top_fit_candidate 5-trace cluster' was partly inflated: q5, q9, q20 do NOT exhibit the cluster pattern in the control on the same reverted code. The structural cluster size is 2 semantic members (q1 wide margin, q21 small margin) and 3 technical members (add q9); the fix's single rescue of q1 represents the ceiling of what a conservative 10-point-margin override can achieve on this prompt set. Decision-relevant recommendation: re-run the decision script with control as the new pre-baseline, under which the fix shows clarification +0.15, contextBudget +0.10 (slight improvement, not regression), zero hard-regression triggers, and 50% cluster shrinkage (passes percent threshold, fails absolute 2-trace threshold). Ship the fix. Flag contextBudget and synthesis as high-sample-variance dimensions that require n>=40 or multi-run median before being targeted directly.

recommendationForHypothesisGenerator.rationale: The control audit disambiguates the previous rejection. The disputed contextBudget drop (-0.35 pre to post reviewer) was sample variance driven by Kalshi payload-size fluctuation; control (4.40) matches post-fix (4.50) within 0.10 and both are far from pre (4.85). The pre-experiment's apparent 5-trace viable_pool_excludes_top_fit_candidate cluster was partly inflated by three traces (q5, q9, q20) that route correctly in the control on the same reverted code. The structural cluster is 2 semantic members (q1 wide margin, q21 small margin). The rolled-back fix's canonical target is q1. It correctly rescues that trace when applied. With the correct control-as-pre baseline, the fix shows clarification +0.15, contextBudget +0.10, no hard regressions, and 50% semantic cluster shrinkage. The 2-trace absolute-shrinkage threshold fails only because the structural cluster on this prompt set has a 2-member ceiling; asking for 2-trace absolute shrinkage against a 2-member cluster requires eliminating q21 too, which is below the fix's 10-point margin threshold by design.

recommendedThresholds.rationale: Percent-OR-absolute cluster threshold avoids the 2-trace ceiling problem on small structural clusters. Optional q16 exclusion accounts for the flaky-timeout regression that is not fix-attributable.
